name: Ingress Tool Transfer - MSI Download from Remote Access Vendor
description: Detects download of MSI installer packages from known remote access software vendors (Tailscale, AnyDesk, TeamViewer, etc.) by non-browser processes. Adversaries transfer legitimate remote access tools to compromised hosts to establish persistent C2 channels.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
query:
sequence by process.entity_id with maxspan=2m
  [process where event.type == "start" and process.name != null]
  [network where event.type == "connection" and
    destination.port == 443 and
    (dns.question.name : ("*.tailscale.com", "*.anydesk.com", "*.teamviewer.com", "*.screenconnect.com", "*.splashtop.com") ) and
    not process.name : ("chrome.exe", "firefox.exe", "msedge.exe", "safari", "opera.exe", "brave.exe", "iexplore.exe", "MicrosoftEdgeUpdate.exe", "GoogleUpdate.exe")]
  [file where event.type == "creation" and file.extension : ("msi", "exe", "zip") and file.size > 1000000]
false_positives: Legitimate IT administrators deploying remote access tools via scripts
Software distribution systems (SCCM, Intune) pushing approved packages
note:
## Triage
- Verify the downloading process identity and parent chain
- Confirm whether the remote access tool is approved in the organization
- Check if the download was initiated by an authorized change request
- Investigate the user account context for signs of compromise

name: Remote Access Software - Silent MSI Installation
description: Detects silent installation of remote access software via msiexec with quiet/passive flags. Adversaries install legitimate remote access tools silently to avoid user interaction and establish covert C2 channels.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  process.name : "msiexec.exe" and
  process.args : ("/i", "-i") and
  process.args : ("/quiet", "/qn", "/passive", "-quiet") and
  process.args : ("*tailscale*", "*anydesk*", "*teamviewer*", "*screenconnect*", "*splashtop*", "*rustdesk*", "*meshagent*")
false_positives: IT deployment scripts installing approved remote access tools
MDM solutions deploying remote support software
note:
## Triage
- Identify the parent process that invoked msiexec
- Verify the MSI package source and digital signature
- Check organizational policy for approved remote access tools
- Look for subsequent network connections to remote access infrastructure

name: Windows Service Creation - OpenSSH Server Installation
description: Detects installation of OpenSSH Server on Windows via Add-WindowsCapability, portable zip extraction to Program Files, or service registration. Adversaries install SSH servers for persistent remote access that blends with legitimate administration traffic.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

mitre_attack.technique_name: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service

query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  (
    (process.name : "powershell.exe" and
      process.args : ("Add-WindowsCapability", "install-sshd.ps1") and
      process.args : ("*OpenSSH*", "*sshd*")
    ) or
    (process.name : "sc.exe" and
      process.args : "create" and
      process.args : "sshd"
    ) or
    (process.name : "powershell.exe" and
      process.args : "Expand-Archive" and
      process.args : "*OpenSSH*"
    )
  )
false_positives: IT administrators enabling OpenSSH for legitimate remote management
Automation tools configuring SSH access during provisioning
note:
## Triage
- Determine if OpenSSH installation was requested through change management
- Check if the installing user account is an authorized administrator
- Review the parent process chain for signs of compromise
- Look for subsequent SSH connections to the host

name: Firewall Rule Creation - SSH Port 22 Inbound
description: Detects creation of Windows firewall rules allowing inbound connections on TCP port 22 (SSH). Adversaries create firewall exceptions to enable remote access through newly installed SSH servers.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

mitre_attack.technique_name: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service
Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify System Firewall

query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  (
    (process.name : "powershell.exe" and
      process.args : "New-NetFirewallRule" and
      process.args : ("22", "*LocalPort*22*")
    ) or
    (process.name : "netsh.exe" and
      process.args : "firewall" and
      process.args : ("22", "ssh")
    )
  )
false_positives: System administrators configuring SSH access for authorized remote management
Infrastructure-as-code tools (Ansible, Puppet) opening SSH port during provisioning
note:
## Triage
- Verify the firewall rule creation was part of an approved change
- Check if SSH service was recently installed on this host
- Look for correlated service installation events
- Determine if SSH is an approved remote access method in the environment

name: Remote Access Software - Tailscale Authentication with Auth Key
description: Detects Tailscale CLI execution with authentication key parameters. Adversaries use pre-generated auth keys to silently join compromised hosts to attacker-controlled tailnets, establishing encrypted mesh VPN tunnels for C2 communication.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  process.name : ("tailscale.exe", "tailscale") and
  process.args : "up" and
  process.args : ("--authkey*", "--auth-key*")
false_positives: Authorized Tailscale deployments joining corporate tailnets
DevOps automation scripts provisioning Tailscale on approved hosts
note:
## Triage
- CRITICAL: This indicates a host joining a Tailscale mesh network with a pre-shared key
- Verify whether Tailscale is an approved tool in the organization
- Identify the auth key source and the tailnet it connects to
- Check for data exfiltration activity following connection establishment
- Investigate the parent process chain for initial access vectors

name: Remote Access Software - Tailscale Network Connections
description: Detects network connections to Tailscale coordination infrastructure (DERP relays, coordination servers) from non-standard processes. These connections indicate a host establishing or maintaining a Tailscale mesh VPN tunnel.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
query:
network where event.type == "connection" and
  (
    dns.question.name : ("*.tailscale.com", "controlplane.tailscale.com", "login.tailscale.com", "*.ts.net") or
    destination.port : (41641, 3478)
  ) and
  not process.name : ("chrome.exe", "firefox.exe", "msedge.exe", "safari")
false_positives: Authorized Tailscale installations communicating with coordination servers
IT staff browsing Tailscale documentation or admin console
note:
## Triage
- Identify the process making the connection
- Check if Tailscale is an approved application
- Look for correlated process creation events for tailscale.exe or tailscaled.exe
- Review for data exfiltration over the established tunnel

name: SSH Lateral Movement - Unexpected SSH Service Activity
description: Detects SSH daemon (sshd) spawning child processes, indicating remote command execution through SSH. On Windows hosts where SSH is not standard, this is a strong indicator of lateral movement or unauthorized remote access.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  process.parent.name : ("sshd.exe", "sshd") and
  process.name : ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe", "bash.exe", "sh", "bash") and
  process.args != null
false_positives: Authorized SSH sessions from system administrators
Automated deployment tools using SSH for remote command execution
note:
## Triage
- Identify the SSH session source IP address
- Verify the authenticating user account is authorized
- Review the commands executed through the SSH session
- Check if SSH is an approved remote access method for this host
- Look for signs of the complete killchain (tool transfer, service install, data staging)

name: Data Exfiltration - Sensitive File Staging and Archive Creation
description: Detects rapid creation of multiple files with sensitive naming patterns (passwords, credentials, keys) followed by archive creation. Adversaries stage sensitive data in a collection directory before compressing and exfiltrating it.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

mitre_attack.technique_name: Archive Collected Data: Archive via Utility

query:
sequence by process.entity_id with maxspan=5m
  [file where event.type == "creation" and
    file.name : ("*password*", "*credential*", "*api_key*", "*secret*", "*private_key*", "*ssh_*key*", "*.pem", "*customer_data*", "*financial*", "*employee*", "*ssn*", "*credit*card*")]
  [file where event.type == "creation" and
    file.extension : ("zip", "7z", "rar", "tar", "gz") and
    file.size > 1024]
false_positives: Backup utilities archiving configuration files
Database export tools creating credential dumps for migration
note:
## Triage
- CRITICAL: This indicates active data staging and preparation for exfiltration
- Identify the process creating the sensitive files
- Determine if the files contain real sensitive data or test data
- Check for outbound network connections following archive creation
- Look for the complete killchain: tool transfer -> service install -> remote access -> data staging

name: Data Exfiltration - Large Archive File Copy or Transfer
description: Detects creation of duplicate archive files or large file copy operations that may indicate data exfiltration staging. Adversaries often copy compressed archives to transfer locations before sending them to external infrastructure.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
query:
file where event.type == "creation" and
  file.extension : ("zip", "7z", "rar", "tar", "gz") and
  file.size > 1048576 and
  file.name : ("*exfil*", "*EXFIL*", "*extract*", "*dump*", "*stolen*", "*loot*", "*data*") and
  not process.name : ("explorer.exe", "7zFM.exe", "WinRAR.exe")
false_positives: Data migration scripts creating named archives
Backup utilities with descriptive archive naming
note:
## Triage
- Review the archive contents if possible
- Identify the process that created the archive
- Check for outbound network connections from the same process
- Correlate with other stages of the killchain

name: Multi-Stage Killchain - Remote Access Tool Deployment with Data Exfiltration
description: Detects the complete killchain of remote access tool deployment followed by service installation and data staging. This high-confidence correlation rule sequences multiple technique indicators to identify sophisticated multi-stage attacks with minimal false positives.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

mitre_attack.technique_name: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service

query:
sequence by host.id with maxspan=1h
  [file where event.type == "creation" and file.extension : ("msi", "exe") and file.size > 1000000]
  [process where event.type == "start" and
    (process.name : "msiexec.exe" and process.args : ("/quiet", "/qn")) or
    (process.name : "powershell.exe" and process.args : ("Add-WindowsCapability", "install-sshd.ps1"))
  ]
  [process where event.type == "start" and
    process.name : ("tailscale.exe", "tailscale") and process.args : "up"]
  [file where event.type == "creation" and file.extension : ("zip", "7z", "rar")]
false_positives: Extremely unlikely - this correlation requires all four stages in sequence within one hour
note:
## Triage
- CRITICAL SEVERITY: This rule fires only when multiple killchain stages are observed in sequence
- Immediately isolate the affected host
- Preserve evidence: memory dump, event logs, network captures
- Identify the initial access vector
- Check for lateral movement to other hosts
- Engage incident response team

name: Remote Access Software - WireGuard Protocol on Non-Standard Process
description: Detects UDP connections on port 41641 (Tailscale WireGuard) or 51820 (standard WireGuard) from processes other than authorized VPN clients. Adversaries leverage WireGuard-based tools like Tailscale to establish encrypted tunnels that bypass network inspection.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder
query:
network where event.type == "connection" and
  network.transport == "udp" and
  destination.port : (41641, 51820, 3478) and
  not process.name : ("wireguard.exe", "wg.exe", "vpnclient.exe", "openvpn.exe")
false_positives: Authorized WireGuard or Tailscale VPN installations
STUN traffic on port 3478 from WebRTC applications
note:
## Triage
- Identify the process generating WireGuard/STUN traffic
- Determine if the destination is a known VPN relay or DERP server
- Check if the host is authorized to use mesh VPN tools
- Correlate with remote access tool installation events

name: Windows Service Tampering - Service Startup Type Changed to Automatic
description: Detects modification of Windows service startup type to automatic via sc.exe or PowerShell Set-Service. Adversaries change service configuration to ensure persistence across reboots, particularly for remote access services like SSH.
author: F0RT1KA Defense Guidance Builder

mitre_attack.technique_name: Create or Modify System Process: Windows Service

query:
process where event.type == "start" and
  (
    (process.name : "sc.exe" and
      process.args : "config" and
      process.args : ("start=", "start=auto")
    ) or
    (process.name : ("powershell.exe", "pwsh.exe") and
      process.args : "Set-Service" and
      process.args : ("Automatic", "*StartupType*Auto*")
    )
  ) and
  not process.parent.name : ("services.exe", "svchost.exe", "msiexec.exe")
false_positives: IT administrators configuring services during maintenance windows
Software installers modifying service startup types
note:
## Triage
- Identify which service startup type was changed
- Verify the change was authorized through change management
- Check the parent process for signs of compromise
- Look for correlated service creation or binary drop events
